_comment: VERCEL DEPLOYMENT — FRONTEND ONLY
IMPORTANT: The XpressPro FX API server (artifacts/api-server) CANNOT run
on Vercel as-is, for three hard architectural reasons:
1. It calls app.listen() — a persistent TCP server. Vercel is serverless;
there is no long-running process. The process is frozen between requests
and torn down after a cold-start timeout.
2. It uses in-memory state (the users/userData Maps in lib/store.ts).
Serverless invocations each get their own isolated memory — any write
in one invocation is invisible to the next. All user data would be lost
between requests.
3. It runs a background sweeper (setInterval in lib/sweeper.ts) that
expires gas-fee deadlines every 30s. setInterval does not run between
invocations in a serverless environment.
CORRECT VERCEL STRATEGY:
- Deploy the FRONTEND apps (NeXTrade, admin-portal) to Vercel as static
React/Vite SPAs. Each is a separate Vercel project pointing at its
subdirectory (artifacts/nextrade/ or artifacts/admin-portal/).
- Keep the API server on Railway, Render, or VPS where a persistent
Node.js process is supported.
- Set VITE_API_BASE_URL (or equivalent) in each Vercel project's env vars
to point to your Railway/Render API URL.
This vercel.json is intentionally left as a stub/documentation file.
To deploy a frontend, create a separate Vercel project via the Vercel
dashboard and set the Root Directory to:
- artifacts/nextrade        (NeXTrade user app)
- artifacts/admin-portal    (XpressPro FX Admin Portal)
If you later rewrite the API server as a Next.js app or add a serverless
adapter (e.g. @vercel/node), update this file accordingly.
Do NOT silently wrap the current Express server in a serverless handler
without first migrating away from in-memory state and the background sweeper.
